name: Alvin Queen
abstract: Alvin Queen is an American-born Swiss jazz drummer born in the Bronx, New York, on August 16, 1950. At 16, he played for Ruth Brown and Don Pullen and with the Wild Bill Davis trio.

interviews.title: Alvin Queen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 23, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]
Alvin Queen interviewed by Monk Row, via Zoom, July 21, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Varner
about_quotes.evidence: I remember, I think I met him... We met but I never played with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Varner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 13, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Tolliver
about_quotes.evidence: I was listening to something from a record you did years later in ’72, it happened to have one of my favorite drummers Alvin Queen on it, and the record was “Impact.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Tolliver interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 6, 2021 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part I
about_quotes.evidence: With Houston Person on saxophone and Alvin Queen on drums and Calvin Hill on bass. And we did it again with the horn.
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Blue
about_quotes.evidence: One of my dearest friends and mentors Dr. Jesse Johnson I met Alvin through him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Blue interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 23, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: we hook up a lot in Europe uh when I go there... Alvin is quite a character, excellent drummer. He fits in with our uh mode also.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Davis
about_quotes.evidence: you’re coming to play with somebody that’s strong and forceful and dynamic as Alvin Queen
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 23, 1997
